discussion_comments.comment_body: Also maybe we should allow passing down of an `options` variable that allows us to selectively disable one of these
IMHO no need to add functions which are deprecated from day 1

At first, I was a bit concerned that removing the deprecated chaining methods like `z.string().md5()` might break the hash validation features. After taking a closer look, I realized those chaining methods aren’t necessary at all direct API calls like `z.md5()` work perfectly fine on their own

[42a79b7](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/4963/commits/42a79b7b60fc2c0a892adde91394030fff94e91b)

So I followed your advice, removed the deprecated methods, and updated the test code to use the direct API approach instead
Thanks so much for reviewing this 😘 this is a feature I really need, so I’d be very grateful if it could be merged...
I'm just a user like you, but I'll review the PR nevertheless.

Having type safety for examples seems like quite a important feature.
```ts
exemplify(example: ParseReturnType<this["_output"]>): this {
```

Or something similar would ensure that required changes to examples are caught if the definition changes.

All shorthand classes (formats) are missing methods for additional refinements.
See #4162  and #4141
This is a blocker for migrating from deprecated methods.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/595acd31-5ded-4ade-b921-561240f1dbb9)

commented_code:

-  B extends string | number | symbol
-> extends ZodType<T["_output"] & BRAND<B>, ZodBrandedDef<T>, T["_input"]> {
+  B extends string | number | symbol,
+  S extends boolean

discussion_comments.comment_body:
this could be 

```suggestion
  S extends boolean = false
```

if we don't want breaking changes
this could go directly to OpenAPI document / components / schemas

The `format` is no longer limited to the pre-defined union of first-party formats, it can be any string. If you need a union of first-party string formats, use `z.core.$ZodStringFormatTypes`.

```ts
export type $ZodStringFormatTypes =
  | $ZodGUID
  | $ZodUUID
  | $ZodEmail
  | $ZodURL
  | $ZodEmoji
  | $ZodNanoID
  | $ZodCUID
  | $ZodCUID2
  | $ZodULID
  | $ZodXID
  | $ZodKSUID
  | $ZodISODateTime
  | $ZodISODate
  | $ZodISOTime
  | $ZodISODuration
  | $ZodIPv4
  | $ZodIPv6
  | $ZodCIDRv4
  | $ZodCIDRv6
  | $ZodBase64
  | $ZodBase64URL
  | $ZodE164
  | $ZodJWT;
```
